discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.topic: Air Tickets Auto Encashment Workflow Configuration
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Article provides complete workflow automation setup process. Navigation map entries provide context for where workflows are accessed and managed within the Air Tickets feature.
PRD describes the broader employee ticket system that air tickets encashment requests integrate with. Navigation map shows employee self-service view for ticket submission and tracking.
PRD defines custom ticket type creation and management capabilities. While air tickets are pre-defined, this system enables extensibility for custom request types.
Single ticket addressing missing notification system for line managers outside standard approval workflows and proxy requesters.
Single ticket addressing complex manual process for moving processed air tickets between payroll periods.
Single ticket addressing lack of unified interface for employees to manage air ticket requests and understand encashment status.
Single ticket addressing loss of pay item history when payroll table rows are disabled.

product_context.title: PRD Employee Tickets
product_context.why_built: Editing and update requests (including air ticket encashment) currently require manual tracking and processing, which is inefficient and error-prone. A centralized ticket system standardizes the request and approval process, eliminating manual tracking and enabling structured workflows for attendance regularization, leave encashment, profile updates, and custom requests.
key_design_decisions: Ticket-based system with standardized request and approval workflows
Default approval routing through Line Manager 1 with configurable alternatives
State transitions: Pending → Approved/Rejected with seamless movement between states
Approval flow changes mid-process: Pending tickets migrate to new flow; approved tickets unaffected
Notification system for CRUD operations via email and mobile push
Role-based access control for viewing, editing, and deleting permissions
Premade ticket types (Attendance, Leave Encashment) with view-only configuration in Release 1-2

product_context.title: PRD: Custom Employee Tickets
product_context.why_built: Organizations have unique internal processes beyond pre-defined ticket types. HR needs to create custom request types with specific fields, approval workflows, and visibility rules. This module enables organizations to digitize any internal process through configurable ticket types rather than relying on email or paper forms.
key_design_decisions: Three-step ticket type creation: Details → Eligibility Rules → Review and Save
Configurable field types: Text, Number, List, Date, Time with mandatory/optional flags
Eligibility rules with filter-based and manual include/exclude logic
Sequential or parallel approval flow configuration with multiple approvers
List Library for reusable dropdowns shared across ticket types
Bilingual support: Arabic and English translations for all labels
Audit trail tracking ticket versions and configuration changes
Built-in ticket types cannot be deleted or change category; custom tickets extend beyond pre-defined types

detected_integrations.description: Primary application for air ticket encashment request creation and processing. Auto encashment workflows route requests to Payroll for calculation and deduction.
Encashment requests are managed and processed through the Finance menu. Payroll deductions and allowance processing occur here.
Admin interface for managing air ticket settings and viewing ticket records. Workflows depend on predefined air ticket approval flows.
Sends notifications to creators and approvers for ticket CRUD operations, workflow triggers, and approval decisions.
Routes tickets through configured approvers. Supports sequential and parallel approval logic with role-based assignment.
Auto-populates employee information in tickets and determines eligibility based on department, office, country, and other attributes.
Source data for attendance regularization tickets. Provides module-level data for ticket review.
Source data for leave encashment tickets. Provides module-level data for ticket review.
Controls who can create, view, edit, delete, and approve tickets. Role-based access determines ticket visibility and actions.
Provides reusable dropdown lists shared across custom ticket types. Supports bilingual (EN/AR) values.

Air ticket encashment transactions flow into payroll processing; issues with moving processed tickets between payroll periods and accessing pay item history indicate tight coupling with payroll period management.
Air ticket requests flow through approval workflows; missing notifications for line managers outside standard approval paths indicates incomplete workflow notification coverage.
Employees need unified interface to manage air ticket requests; current implementation lacks comprehensive self-service capabilities across policy variations.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Create Air Tickets Auto Encashment Workflow
steps: Navigate to Settings and access Workflows section
Click to create a new workflow
Select the Air Tickets Auto Encashment recipe from available templates
Define the workflow trigger by setting the date and time (hour-level precision only)
Configure encashment criteria by selecting appropriate encashment options
Set request details: choose Bayzat Payroll as the application
Select the encashment request type (fully approved or requiring approvals)
Assign the employee who will receive the encashment request
Save the workflow configuration
Verify workflow activation and monitor activity log
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Workflow is created, activated, and automatically generates air ticket encashment requests at the specified trigger time according to defined criteria. Requests are routed to the assigned employee and integrated with Bayzat Payroll for processing.
rules_and_constraints: Time selection is limited to hour-level precision; minute selector is non-functional
Time is based on UAE or KSA account location settings
Must select an assignee (employee) for the encashment request
Can choose between fully approved requests or requests requiring approvals
Workflow depends on predefined air ticket approval flows being configured
Encashment request type selection must be appropriate for the workflow trigger
edge_cases: If minute selector appears non-functional, use only hour selection for trigger timing
Workflow activation may fail if air ticket approval flows are not properly configured
Time zone differences between account location (UAE/KSA) and user location may affect trigger execution

source_articles.title: Air Tickets Auto Encashments via Workflows

what_to_do.task: Submit Air Ticket Encashment Request as Employee
steps: Navigate to My Requests section in the platform
Select My Tickets or My Air Tickets view
Locate and select the air ticket encashment request type
Fill in all required fields with accurate information
Attach any supporting documentation if required
Review the request details before submission
Submit the ticket
Track the ticket status (Pending, Approved, or Rejected) in your ticket list
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Air ticket encashment request is submitted successfully and routed to the configured approver (typically Line Manager 1). Employee receives confirmation and can track the request status through the ticket system.
rules_and_constraints: All mandatory fields must be completed before submission
Request is routed through the default approval flow (Line Manager 1) unless otherwise configured
Employees can only view and manage their own tickets
Ticket state transitions follow: Pending → Approved/Rejected
edge_cases: If approval flow is changed mid-process, pending tickets migrate to the new flow automatically
New employees must be added to the air ticket type by admin before they can submit requests
Notifications may be sent via email or mobile push depending on employee notification preferences

what_to_do.task: Review and Approve Air Ticket Encashment Requests as Manager
steps: Navigate to Requests section in the platform
Select Employee Tickets or Air Tickets view
Search, filter, or sort tickets by employee name, ticket ID, or ticket type
Open the air ticket encashment request to review details
Review module-level data and any attached documentation
Add comments or notes if needed for clarification
Make approval decision: Approve or Reject the request
If rejecting, provide comments explaining the reason
Submit the approval decision
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Air ticket encashment request is reviewed and approved or rejected. Creator and approvers receive notifications of the decision. Approved requests proceed to Bayzat Payroll for processing; rejected requests return to Pending state with feedback.
rules_and_constraints: Managers can view all tickets from their direct and indirect reportees
Approval routing follows configured approval flows
Approved tickets cannot be reverted to pending state
Comments are required when rejecting requests
edge_cases: If approval flow changes after a ticket is partially approved, pending approvals migrate to the new flow
Approved tickets are unaffected by approval flow changes
Managers may create tickets on behalf of employees if configured by admin

what_to_do.task: Configure Custom Ticket Types for Organization-Specific Requests
steps: Navigate to Settings and access Tickets configuration
Create or select a category (e.g., Payroll, IT, Attendance) with English and Arabic names
Create a new ticket type: enter name, description, category, priority, and assigned roles
Add ticket fields: select field type (Text, Number, List, Date, Time) and configure properties
Mark fields as mandatory or optional and set character limits
Provide English and Arabic translations for all field labels
Set eligibility rules by Department, Office, Country, Gender, or other criteria
Configure approval flow: choose sequential or parallel logic and assign approvers
Review and save the ticket type configuration
Monitor ticket usage and audit trail for configuration changes
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Custom ticket type is created and available for employees to submit. Requests are routed through configured approval workflows. Admins can track ticket versions and configuration changes through audit trail.
rules_and_constraints: Cannot delete default or built-in categories or system lists
Cannot delete categories with associated ticket types
Cannot deactivate lists in use by active tickets
Built-in ticket types cannot change category or be deleted
At least one role must be assigned who can create the ticket type
Arabic and English translations are required for all labels
Editing ticket types already in use displays a warning
Deactivating fields in use displays a warning but retains the field in existing tickets
edge_cases: If a category is in use, editing displays a warning; deletion is prevented
If a field is in use, editing or deactivating displays a warning; field is retained in existing tickets
Eligibility conflicts: manual include/exclude rules override filter-based rules
Existing tickets retain previous field versions after ticket type edits (version mismatch labeled as 'older config')
Priority defaults to Medium if not specified during creation

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Missing Line Manager Notifications in Air Ticket Approval
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Line managers are not automatically notified or tagged when air ticket requests are processed outside the standard approval workflow. Proxy requesters and managers raising requests for employees do not receive automated notifications about ticket processing status.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Complex Payroll Period Adjustment for Processed Air Tickets
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Users cannot easily move processed (encashed) air tickets between payroll periods. The current workflow requires manual reopening of payroll periods, deletion of transactions, and status changes to pending, creating a complex and error-prone process.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Limited Employee Self-Service for Air Ticket Management
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Employees lack a unified interface to manage air ticket requests across different policy conditions. Employees cannot fully interact with or understand their air ticket encashment status in certain scenarios.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Pay Item History Inaccessible When Payroll Table Disabled
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: When payroll table rows are disabled, users cannot access the history popup for pay items, preventing retrieval of historical compensation information and interrupting the workflow.

gaps_identified: Finance Manager: No tasks identified. Finance managers process payroll and calculate settlements but are not explicitly covered in air ticket workflows. Consider adding task for Finance Manager to process approved encashment requests in Payroll module.

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
